title: Maui 10-Day Beach Getaway
metaTitle: Maui 10-Day Beach Getaway | Flights, Hotels & Tours Included
metaDescription: Book a 10-day trip to Maui, United States: round-trip flights, hotel, breakfast, transfers, and guided tours including Road to Hana guided tour and Haleakalā National Park sunrise entry. Prices from $2907 per person (estimate).
highlights: Snorkeling and diving at Molokini Crater
Oceanfront suite at the Four Seasons Wailea
Private sunset dinner on the beach
overview: Spend 10 days discovering Maui, United States — Tropical climate with warm temperatures year-round; a drier season (Apr-Oct) and a slightly wetter, cooler season (Nov-Mar), with conditions varying sharply between the windward and leeward coasts and by elevation. April-May and September-October shoulder seasons for good weather, lower prices, and thinner crowds. This itinerary blends Road to Hana guided tour, Haleakalā National Park sunrise entry, Baldwin Beach Park with free time to explore at your own pace, backed by round-trip flights, handpicked accommodation, and a dedicated travel consultant from enquiry through departure.
included: Round-trip economy-class airfare from your selected origin city (upgrade to premium economy or business available)
9 nights accommodation in your selected category (Budget, Mid-range, Premium, or Luxury)
Daily local transport as per the itinerary
Guided tours including Road to Hana guided tour, Haleakalā National Park sunrise entry, Baldwin Beach Park
All entrance fees for attractions and activities listed in the itinerary
Applicable taxes and OTA service fee
Dedicated travel consultant support from booking through departure
notIncluded: Visa fees and travel document costs (see Visa Information)
Travel insurance (strongly recommended, available as an add-on)
Lunches and dinners except where noted in the itinerary
Optional activities not listed in the day-by-day itinerary
Airline seat selection and baggage upgrades
Any fare difference for premium economy or business class upgrades

itinerary.title: Arrive in Maui
itinerary.description: Land at Kahului Airport (OGG) and take your included private transfer to your hotel. After settling in, ease into the trip with a relaxed visit to Road to Hana guided tour — Guided version of the famous scenic drive. Round off the evening with a welcome dinner introducing you to local flavors.

itinerary.title: Maui: Haleakalā National Park sunrise entry & Old Lahaina Luau
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Haleakalā National Park sunrise entry — Reserved sunrise viewing atop the volcano summit. In the afternoon, continue to Old Lahaina Luau — Traditional Hawaiian feast and dance performance. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Maui: Maui Ocean Center & Molokini Crater snorkeling boat trip
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Maui Ocean Center — Aquarium focused on Hawaiian reef life. In the afternoon, continue to Molokini Crater snorkeling boat trip — Boat snorkel excursion to a crescent-shaped crater reef. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Road to Hana
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Road to Hana, a popular side trip from Maui. Return to Maui in the evening with time free to explore on your own.
activities: Guided day trip: Road to Hana

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Wai'anapanapa State Park black sand beach — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.
activities: Wai'anapanapa State Park black sand beach

itinerary.title: Maui: Whale watching cruise (winter season) & Skyline Eco-Adventures zipline
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Whale watching cruise (winter season) — Humpback whale watching boat tour. In the afternoon, continue to Skyline Eco-Adventures zipline — Canopy zipline tour in upcountry Maui. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Haleakalā National Park sunrise
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Haleakalā National Park sunrise, a popular side trip from Maui. Return to Maui in the evening with time free to explore on your own.
activities: Guided day trip: Haleakalā National Park sunrise

itinerary.title: Maui: Iao Valley State Park & Sunrise bike ride down Haleakalā
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Iao Valley State Park — Park with the iconic Iao Needle rock formation. In the afternoon, continue to Sunrise bike ride down Haleakalā — Guided cycling descent from the summit. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Kula lavender farms upcountry — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.

itinerary.title: Departure Day
itinerary.description: Enjoy a final morning at leisure — perhaps one last visit to Surf lesson group class or some souvenir shopping — before your private transfer to OGG for your departure flight.

travelTips: Book Haleakalā sunrise viewing reservations online in advance — a permit system limits entry
Fill up on gas before starting the Road to Hana, as stations are scarce
Start the Road to Hana early to avoid oncoming tour traffic on narrow one-lane bridges
Respect "kapu" (forbidden/off-limits) signs at sacred and private sites
Pace yourself — this itinerary includes free time so you are not rushing between every attraction.
packingList: Copies of booking confirmations and travel insurance
Lightweight, breathable clothing and a reusable water bottle
Reef-safe, mineral-based sunscreen, required by Hawaii law near reefs
Lightweight breathable clothing plus a warm layer for the chilly Haleakalā summit sunrise
Water shoes for rocky snorkel entries
A reusable water bottle for long Road to Hana drives
thingsToDo: Snorkeling and diving at Molokini Crater
Sunrise bike descent from Haleakalā summit
thingsToAvoid: Removing lava rocks or sand from beaches — considered bad luck and culturally disrespectful
Swimming at unpatrolled beaches with strong shore breaks or currents
Driving the Road to Hana too fast on blind curves
Using non-reef-safe sunscreen, which is restricted by Hawaii state law
topRestaurants: Leoda's Kitchen and Pie Shop — Bakery and casual American ($)
Paia Fish Market — Fresh fish tacos and plates ($$)
Duo Steak and Seafood — Steak and seafood ($$$)
Cafe des Amis — Crepes and Indian-inspired wraps ($)
Ono Organic Farms roadside stand — Fresh tropical fruit ($)
hiddenGems: Wai'anapanapa State Park black sand beach
adventureActivities: Snorkeling and diving at Molokini Crater
Sunrise bike descent from Haleakalā summit
luxuryExperiences: Oceanfront suite at the Four Seasons Wailea
Private helicopter tour over the Hana coastline and volcano
honeymoonExperiences: Private sunset dinner on the beach
budgetExperiences: Free snorkeling at public beaches like Kapalua Bay
Self-drive Road to Hana with free roadside stops
Baldwin Beach Park for a free beach day
Iao Valley State Park short hike

festivalCalendar.name: Ka Hula Piko (statewide Hawaiian culture celebration)

localEtiquette: Remove shoes before entering a home.
Respect "aloha" and "mālama 'āina" — caring for the land — in how you treat nature.
Never take lava rocks or sand from beaches; it's considered bad luck and disrespectful.
Yield to Hawaiian cultural practices at sacred sites (heiau).
Use only reef-safe, mineral-based sunscreen, required by Hawaii state law near reefs.
localTransportGuide: A rental car is essential on Maui, as public transportation (the Maui Bus) is limited and doesn't reach most resort areas or the Road to Hana. Roads can be narrow and winding, especially en route to Hana, so allow a full day for the round trip.
bestTimeToVisit: April-May and September-October shoulder seasons for good weather, lower prices, and thinner crowds.
weather: Tropical climate with warm temperatures year-round; a drier season (Apr-Oct) and a slightly wetter, cooler season (Nov-Mar), with conditions varying sharply between the windward and leeward coasts and by elevation.
visaInformation: Travelers from Visa Waiver Program countries need an approved ESTA before flying, valid for stays up to 90 days; other nationalities require a B-2 tourist visa. Hawaii is a US state, so standard US entry rules apply.
averageFlightTime: ~15.7h from major hubs (estimate; varies by origin — see Suggested Flights)
cancellationPolicy: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.
termsAndConditions: Pricing shown is per person based on the traveler type and tier selected and is an estimate pending live fare and rate confirmation.
Passports must be valid for at least 6 months beyond the return date for most destinations — confirm the exact requirement for your nationality.
Itinerary order may change due to weather, local conditions, or attraction closures; inclusions remain equivalent.
Solo travelers incur a single-room supplement, reflected in the Solo pricing tier.
This package does not include international travel insurance unless explicitly listed under Included.
Prices shown in a currency other than USD are approximate conversions at the rate noted and are not a payment quote.

faqs.question: Do I need a visa to visit Maui?
faqs.answer: Travelers from Visa Waiver Program countries need an approved ESTA before flying, valid for stays up to 90 days; other nationalities require a B-2 tourist visa. Hawaii is a US state, so standard US entry rules apply.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit Maui?
faqs.answer: April-May and September-October shoulder seasons for good weather, lower prices, and thinner crowds. Tropical climate with warm temperatures year-round; a drier season (Apr-Oct) and a slightly wetter, cooler season (Nov-Mar), with conditions varying sharply between the windward and leeward coasts and by elevation.

faqs.question: What's included in this package?
faqs.answer: Round-trip airfare, accommodation, daily breakfast, airport transfers, daily local transport, and guided tours to the attractions listed in the itinerary. See the Included / Not Included sections for full detail.

faqs.question: Can the itinerary be customized?
faqs.answer: Yes — this itinerary is a starting point. Your travel consultant can add, remove, or reorder activities, extend your stay, or upgrade your hotel category before you book.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.

faqs.question: Is Maui safe for tourists?
faqs.answer: Maui is generally safe for tourists who take standard precautions — avoid poorly lit areas at night, keep valuables secure, and use licensed transport.

faqs.question: What currency should I bring to Maui?
faqs.answer: The local currency is USD. Cards and mobile payments are widely accepted, and ATMs are easy to find.

faqs.question: Is this package good for a honeymoon or family trip?
faqs.answer: Yes — Maui is one of our most-booked honeymoon destinations, especially the Private sunset dinner on the beach experience.

reviews.author: Chen Khan
reviews.title: Exceeded our expectations
reviews.body: Booked this as a solo trip and it was well paced. Makena Beach ("Big Beach") in particular stood out. A couple of the transfers ran a little late but nothing that spoiled the trip.

reviews.author: Sofia Silva
reviews.title: Smooth from start to finish
reviews.body: Booked this as a couple trip and it was well paced. Twin Falls in particular stood out. A couple of the transfers ran a little late but nothing that spoiled the trip.

reviews.author: Mia Botha
reviews.title: Exceeded our expectations
reviews.body: Everything was handled for us — flights, hotel, transfers, and tours to Makena Beach ("Big Beach") and Old Lahaina Luau. Great communication from the consultant before departure too.

reviews.author: Anna Fernandez
reviews.title: Amazing itinerary, zero stress
reviews.body: Maui is stunning and this package covered the essentials well, especially Maui Ocean Center. The hotel could have been a little closer to the center but transport was easy.

reviews.author: Ethan van Dijk
reviews.title: Highly recommend this package
reviews.body: Everything was handled for us — flights, hotel, transfers, and tours to Old Lahaina Luau and Old Lahaina Luau. Great communication from the consultant before departure too.

reviews.author: Ava Sharma
reviews.title: Smooth from start to finish
reviews.body: Maui Ocean Center alone was worth the trip, but combined with Lahaina Historic District this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.
